combined_summary: The most critical immediate actions relate to solidifying the logistical foundation and mitigating compliance failures before the Q3 national rollout. First, securing the external regulatory approval timeline for cleaning agents (Expert 2, Action 1) is non-negotiable, as reintroducing uncertified food-contact material invalidates the core environmental benefit and risks legal jeopardy. Second, formalizing the SLA agreements with municipal recycling stations (Expert 1, Action 2) is essential, as the agreed 60% volume split relies entirely on their capacity and staffing commitments, which must be locked down by 2026-07-31 to handle the high logistical load assumed by the 'Builder' strategy. Third, defining the exact QA triage process, including personnel and cleaning parameters (Expert 1, Action 1), must happen immediately to ensure the centralized hubs are functional and staffed correctly before the pilot phase concludes in Q2, directly controlling the cost-per-unit recovery.

feedback.title: Define Central Hub QA Triage Specification
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Conduct site surveys at three predefined major population center areas (Copenhagen, Aarhus, Odense analogues) by 2026-03-25 to establish baseline site leasing costs for the three centralized quality assessment hubs; Develop the intake protocol flow chart specifying required personnel headcount (cleaning/sorting operatives) per hub based on an expected throughput of 20,000 crates per week; Define the precise cleaning process validation requiring specific detergent volumes and required dwell time (e.g., 15 minutes wash cycle at 60°C) to ensure plastic integrity before reintroduction.

feedback.title: Formalize 60/40 Channel Load Agreements
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Draft legally binding Service Level Agreements (SLAs) for municipal recycling stations committing to handling at least 60% of projected volume (equivalent to 54,000 crates/month during peak Q3/Q4) with a designated intake window between 08:00 and 17:00 local time, due for signature by 2026-07-31; Issue binding contracts to Salling Group, Coop Danmark, and Rema 1000 mandating required floor space allocation (minimum 3 square meters, clearly marked) for designated drop-off points in 70% of their high-volume stores by 2026-06-01; For the pilot (Q2), deploy temporary mobile collection units in 5 distinct, high-density urban postcodes for 4 weeks to empirically test logistical friction against the modeled 60/40 split and adjust Hub location sourcing by 2026-05-15.

feedback.title: Calibrate Incentive Tiers and Budget Guardrails
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Finalize the tiered incentive structure by determining the exact trigger volume for the secondary 3 DKK incentive tier (to manage 1.35M DKK ceiling), setting the threshold no higher than 100,000 total returns, officially documenting this budget cap decision by 2026-03-15; Prepare the initial batch of 10,000 high-visibility identification tags/stickers (Decision 3, Option 1 alternative) for immediate deployment during the Q2 pilot, as a reliable backup to the visual guide communication; Initiate procurement for 150 standardized, stackable collection containers (minimum 150 Liter capacity, clearly branded) for deployment across the three QA hubs and testing at 10 pilot locations by 2026-06-30, necessary for bulk handling.

feedback.title: Establish Procurement Link for 2027 Offset
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Design the mandatory 'Ready-to-Deploy Stock Report' format specifying required fields: Reusable Count, Triage Failure Count (>damaged for recycling), and Total Cleaned Ready Volume (DKK); Schedule mandatory monthly synchronization meetings between the QA Hub Logistics Lead and Arla Procurement for the 3rd Tuesday of every month, beginning September 3rd, 2026, to guarantee alignment with 2027 production planning; Procure initial quantities of repair/sanitation materials sufficient for 50,000 crates (e.g., 500 liters industrial sanitizing agent certified for HDPE contact, 1,000 specialized repair patches) to cover Q3 operational buffer, sourcing immediately due to Q3 launch dependency.

feedback.title: Secure Food-Contact Plastic Certification Deadlines
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Submit the chosen industrial cleaning agent and thermal process parameters (per Expert 1's specification) to the Danish Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for documented reuse certification clearance no later than 2026-04-15; Establish a mandatory internal hold point post-pilot (2026-07-10) where no crates can be reintroduced to the supply chain without documented confirmation (signed certificate copy retained) that cleaning protocols meet EPF food-grade standards; Immediately allocate DKK 150,000 from the Logistics Budget (1.8M estimate) to cover expedited third-party external lab testing required for validation, due to the urgency of the Q3 rollout date.

feedback.title: Insulate Stakeholders from Provocative Marketing
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Draft and circulate a 'Pre-Approval Addendum' to all supermarket groups (Salling, Coop, Rema 1000) by 2026-03-20, explicitly detailing the humor/shock elements of the 'Garden Furniture' narrative (Lever 4, Option 2) for written sign-off on collateral placement prior to printing; Establish a dedicated, monitored feedback channel for supermarket management staff specifically to flag in-store operational friction or negative customer sentiment related to crate drop-off procedures, initiating a response protocol within 4 hours for escalation paths defined by Risk 2 mitigation; Immediately prepare baseline public relations statements (max 300 words) ready for deployment by 2026-05-01 to re-frame negative social media commentary solely around the quantifiable 'children's nutrition' support (5 DKK per crate) should the provocative angle backfire.

feedback.title: Define Cross-Contamination Triage Protocol
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Develop an airtight, standardized inspection checklist (Yes/No criteria) for use at the centralized hubs, specifically flagging evidence of non-food debris (e.g., soil contamination, garden waste) which automatically routes the crate for destructive industrial recycling (not simple plastics recycling), due by 2026-05-20; Implement a 5% random audit buffer within the 1.8M DKK logistics budget specifically earmarked to cover unexpected operational overtime costs associated with surge contamination (Risk 4 contingency), releasing funds only upon verification of contamination rates above 15%; Contract external environmental consultants by 2026-06-15 to verify the external tracking mechanism for calculating CO2 tonnage reduction is compliant with current Danish EPA standards for material substitution accounting.

feedback.title: Lock Down Municipal Capacity Coefficients
feedback.description: To initiate this project, you must: Include mandatory penalty clauses (deduction of 5,000 DKK per scheduled day staffing falls below the minimum FTE coefficient detailed in the (missing) SLA draft) in all municipal agreements, to be signed by 2026-07-31; Design and mail physical, laminated quick-reference guides detailing the sorting triage (Arla reusable vs. contamination vs. look-alike) to the 50 largest municipal stations by 2026-07-01, independent of full staff training completion; Immediately notify municipal waste authorities that cleaning agent residual testing confirms non-toxicity for incidental ground contact during transport/storage, mitigating potential localized environmental complaints during Q3 collection peaks.
